title.es: Normal vs Realidad en Retornos Financieros
title.en: Normal vs Reality in Financial Returns

summary_es: Este modulo demuestra formalmente que los retornos financieros no siguen una distribucion Normal. Se generan retornos sinteticos tipo S&P 500 con colas pesadas y asimetria negativa, y se aplican tres tests de normalidad: Jarque-Bera, Shapiro-Wilk y Anderson-Darling. Se usa el QQ-plot como diagnostico visual clave. Se ajustan distribuciones Normal y Student-t via MLE y se comparan con AIC/BIC, demostrando que Student-t es estrictamente superior. Se cuantifica el impacto: el VaR Normal subestima el riesgo en 20-40%, y los eventos de 4+ sigma son 80-100x mas frecuentes que lo que la Normal predice. Se presentan las implicaciones para pricing de opciones, gestion de riesgo y cumplimiento regulatorio.
summary_en: This module formally demonstrates that financial returns do not follow a Normal distribution. Synthetic S&P 500-like returns with fat tails and negative skewness are generated, and three normality tests are applied: Jarque-Bera, Shapiro-Wilk, and Anderson-Darling. QQ-plots serve as the key visual diagnostic. Normal and Student-t distributions are fitted via MLE and compared with AIC/BIC, showing Student-t is strictly superior. The impact is quantified: Normal VaR underestimates risk by 20-40%, and 4+ sigma events are 80-100x more frequent than Normal predicts. Implications for options pricing, risk management, and regulatory compliance are presented.
learning_objectives_es: Demostrar formalmente la no-normalidad de retornos con 3 tests estadisticos
Interpretar QQ-plots para diagnosticar fat tails y asimetria
Ajustar Student-t vs Normal via MLE y comparar con AIC/BIC
Cuantificar el error de VaR por asumir normalidad
Construir la tabla de eventos extremos: observados vs esperados
learning_objectives_en: Formally demonstrate non-normality of returns with 3 statistical tests
Interpret QQ-plots to diagnose fat tails and asymmetry
Fit Student-t vs Normal via MLE and compare with AIC/BIC
Quantify VaR error from assuming normality
Build the extreme events table: observed vs expected

parameters.name: Non-Gaussian Financial Returns
parameters.origin_phrase: Three formal tests reject normality of financial returns with p << 0.001

parameters.name: Specification Error
parameters.origin_phrase: Assuming normality is a specification error that underestimates risk by 20-40%

parameters.name: Model Robustness
parameters.origin_phrase: Student-t with nu~4 captures tail risk 100x better than Normal
